extraction.one_line_summary: ASGREEN Kasper won from a late breakaway at 8 seconds with 700m to go, holding off the sprinters after Philippsen blocked early bridge attempts.
tactical_patterns: Philippsen blocked Inchcorn's early bridge attempt to protect breakaway tactics, signaling Quick Step's alternative stage-win strategy when sprint trains absent.
Sprint teams collectively refused full commitment in final 5km with headwind/technical finish, allowing 4-man break to survive from 8sec at 700m.
Quick Step used Declercq's leadout skills for breakaway rider Asgreen instead of sprint train, maximizing veteran domestique value late-tour.

rider_signals.PHILIPPSEN Jasper: neutral: blocked Inchcorn bridge despite 4 prior stage wins, suggesting Quick Step prioritized any team win over fifth sprint victory
rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: positive: converted leadout horsepower into solo breakaway win after 18-day wait, showing versatility beyond classics specialty
rider_signals.CAMPENAERTS Victor: positive: massive final turn from breakaway kept 4-man group alive through 700m despite peloton closing to 8sec
